modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c
1/* Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
2 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
3 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
4 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
5 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
6 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
7 *
8 *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9 *
10 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14 * limitations under the License.
15 */
16
17/*
18 * Author: mod_file_cache by Bill Stoddard <stoddard apache.org>
19 *         Based on mod_mmap_static by Dean Gaudet <dgaudet arctic.org>
20 *
21 * v0.01: initial implementation
22 */ view japanese
23
24/*
25    Documentation:
26
27    Some sites have a set of static files that are really busy, and
28    change infrequently (or even on a regular schedule). Save time
29    by caching open handles to these files. This module, unlike
30    mod_mmap_static, caches open file handles, not file content.
31    On systems (like Windows) with heavy system call overhead and
32    that have an efficient sendfile implementation, caching file handles
33    offers several advantages over caching content. First, the file system
34    can manage the memory, allowing infrequently hit cached files to
35    be paged out. Second, since caching open handles does not consume
36    significant resources, it will be possible to enable an AutoLoadCache
37    feature where static files are dynamically loaded in the cache
38    as the server runs. On systems that have file change notification,
39    this module can be enhanced to automatically garbage collect
40    cached files that change on disk.
41
42    This module should work on Unix systems that have sendfile. Place
43    cachefile directives into your configuration to direct files to
44    be cached.
45
46        cachefile /path/to/file1
47        cachefile /path/to/file2
48        ...
49
50    These files are only cached when the server is restarted, so if you
51    change the list, or if the files are changed, then you'll need to
52    restart the server.
53
54    To reiterate that point:  if the files are modified *in place*
55    without restarting the server you may end up serving requests that
56    are completely bogus.  You should update files by unlinking the old
57    copy and putting a new copy in place.
58
59    There's no such thing as inheriting these files across vhosts or
60    whatever... place the directives in the main server only.
61
62    Known problems:
63
64    Don't use Alias or RewriteRule to move these files around...  unless
65    you feel like paying for an extra stat() on each request.  This is
66    a deficiency in the Apache API that will hopefully be solved some day.
67    The file will be served out of the file handle cache, but there will be
68    an extra stat() that's a waste.
69*/ view japanese
70
71#include "apr.h"
72
73#if !(APR_HAS_SENDFILE || APR_HAS_MMAP)
74#error mod_file_cache only works on systems with APR_HAS_SENDFILE or APR_HAS_MMAP
75#endif
76
77#include "apr_mmap.h"
78#include "apr_strings.h"
79#include "apr_hash.h"
80#include "apr_buckets.h"
81
82#define APR_WANT_STRFUNC
83#include "apr_want.h"
84
85#if APR_HAVE_SYS_TYPES_H
86#include <sys/types.h>
87#endif
88
89#include "httpd.h"
90#include "http_config.h"
91#include "http_log.h"
92#include "http_protocol.h"
93#include "http_request.h"
94#include "http_core.h"
95
96module AP_MODULE_DECLARE_DATA file_cache_module;
97
98typedef struct {
99#if APR_HAS_SENDFILE
100    apr_file_t *file;
101#endif
102    const char *filename;
103    apr_finfo_t finfo;
104    int is_mmapped;
105#if APR_HAS_MMAP
106    apr_mmap_t *mm;
107#endif
108    char mtimestr[APR_RFC822_DATE_LEN];
109    char sizestr[21];   /* big enough to hold any 64-bit file size + null */ view japanese
110} a_file;
111
112typedef struct {
113    apr_hash_t *fileht;
114} a_server_config;
115
116
117static void *create_server_config(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
118{
119    a_server_config *sconf = apr_palloc(p, sizeof(*sconf));
120
121    sconf->fileht = apr_hash_make(p);
122    return sconf;
123}
124
125static void cache_the_file(cmd_parms *cmd, const char *filename, int mmap)
126{
127    a_server_config *sconf;
128    a_file *new_file;
129    a_file tmp;
130    apr_file_t *fd = NULL;
131    apr_status_t rc;
132    const char *fspec;
133
134    fspec = ap_server_root_relative(cmd->pool, filename);
135    if (!fspec) {
136        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, APR_EBADPATH, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00794)
137                     "invalid file path "
138                     "%s, skipping", filename);
139        return;
140    }
141    if ((rc = apr_stat(&tmp.finfo, fspec, APR_FINFO_MIN,
142                                 cmd->temp_pool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
143        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, rc, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00795)
144                     "unable to stat(%s), skipping", fspec);
145        return;
146    }
147    if (tmp.finfo.filetype != APR_REG) {
148        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00796)
149                     "%s isn't a regular file, skipping", fspec);
150        return;
151    }
152    if (tmp.finfo.size > AP_MAX_SENDFILE) {
153        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00797)
154                     "%s is too large to cache, skipping", fspec);
155        return;
156    }
157
158    rc = apr_file_open(&fd, fspec, APR_READ | APR_BINARY | APR_XTHREAD,
159                       APR_OS_DEFAULT, cmd->pool);
160    if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
161        ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, rc, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00798)
162                     "unable to open(%s, O_RDONLY), skipping", fspec);
163        return;
164    }
165    apr_file_inherit_set(fd);
166
167    /* WooHoo, we have a file to put in the cache */ view japanese
168    new_file = apr_pcalloc(cmd->pool, sizeof(a_file));
169    new_file->finfo = tmp.finfo;
170
171#if APR_HAS_MMAP
172    if (mmap) {
173        /* MMAPFile directive. MMAP'ing the file
174         * XXX: APR_HAS_LARGE_FILES issue; need to reject this request if
175         * size is greater than MAX(apr_size_t) (perhaps greater than 1M?).
176         */ view japanese
177        if ((rc = apr_mmap_create(&new_file->mm, fd, 0,
178                                  (apr_size_t)new_file->finfo.size,
179                                  APR_MMAP_READ, cmd->pool)) != APR_SUCCESS) {
180            apr_file_close(fd);
181            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, rc, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00799)
182                         "unable to mmap %s, skipping", filename);
183            return;
184        }
185        apr_file_close(fd);
186        new_file->is_mmapped = TRUE;
187    }
188#endif
189#if APR_HAS_SENDFILE
190    if (!mmap) {
191        /* CacheFile directive. Caching the file handle */ view japanese
192        new_file->is_mmapped = FALSE;
193        new_file->file = fd;
194    }
195#endif
196
197    new_file->filename = fspec;
198    apr_rfc822_date(new_file->mtimestr, new_file->finfo.mtime);
199    apr_snprintf(new_file->sizestr, sizeof new_file->sizestr, "%" APR_OFF_T_FMT, new_file->finfo.size);
200
201    sconf = ap_get_module_config(cmd->server->module_config, &file_cache_module);
202    apr_hash_set(sconf->fileht, new_file->filename, strlen(new_file->filename), new_file);
203
204}
205
206static const char *cachefilehandle(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy, const char *filename)
207{
208#if APR_HAS_SENDFILE
209    cache_the_file(cmd, filename, 0);
210#else
211    /* Sendfile not supported by this OS */ view japanese
212    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00800)
213                 "unable to cache file: %s. Sendfile is not supported on this OS", filename);
214#endif
215    return NULL;
216}
217static const char *cachefilemmap(cmd_parms *cmd, void *dummy, const char *filename)
218{
219#if APR_HAS_MMAP
220    cache_the_file(cmd, filename, 1);
221#else
222    /* MMAP not supported by this OS */ view japanese
223    ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_WARNING, 0, cmd->server, APLOGNO(00801)
224                 "unable to cache file: %s. MMAP is not supported by this OS", filename);
225#endif
226    return NULL;
227}
228
229static int file_cache_post_config(apr_pool_t *p, apr_pool_t *plog,
230                                   apr_pool_t *ptemp, server_rec *s)
231{
232    /* Hummm, anything to do here? */ view japanese
233    return OK;
234}
235
236/* If it's one of ours, fill in r->finfo now to avoid extra stat()... this is a
237 * bit of a kludge, because we really want to run after core_translate runs.
238 */ view japanese
239static int file_cache_xlat(request_rec *r)
240{
241    a_server_config *sconf;
242    a_file *match;
243    int res;
244
245    sconf = ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config, &file_cache_module);
246
247    /* we only operate when at least one cachefile directive was used */ view japanese
248    if (!apr_hash_count(sconf->fileht)) {
249        return DECLINED;
250    }
251
252    res = ap_core_translate(r);
253    if (res != OK || !r->filename) {
254        return res;
255    }
256
257    /* search the cache */ view japanese
258    match = (a_file *) apr_hash_get(sconf->fileht, r->filename, APR_HASH_KEY_STRING);
259    if (match == NULL)
260        return DECLINED;
261
262    /* pass search results to handler */ view japanese
263    ap_set_module_config(r->request_config, &file_cache_module, match);
264
265    /* shortcircuit the get_path_info() stat() calls and stuff */ view japanese
266    r->finfo = match->finfo;
267    return OK;
268}
269
270static int mmap_handler(request_rec *r, a_file *file)
271{
272#if APR_HAS_MMAP
273    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
274    apr_bucket *b;
275    apr_mmap_t *mm;
276    apr_bucket_brigade *bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool, c->bucket_alloc);
277
278    apr_mmap_dup(&mm, file->mm, r->pool);
279    b = apr_bucket_mmap_create(mm, 0, (apr_size_t)file->finfo.size,
280                               c->bucket_alloc);
281    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, b);
282    b = apr_bucket_eos_create(c->bucket_alloc);
283    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, b);
284
285    if (ap_pass_brigade(r->output_filters, bb) != APR_SUCCESS)
286        return AP_FILTER_ERROR;
287#endif
288    return OK;
289}
290
291static int sendfile_handler(request_rec *r, a_file *file)
292{
293#if APR_HAS_SENDFILE
294    conn_rec *c = r->connection;
295    apr_bucket *b;
296    apr_bucket_brigade *bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool, c->bucket_alloc);
297
298    apr_brigade_insert_file(bb, file->file, 0, file->finfo.size, r->pool);
299
300    b = apr_bucket_eos_create(c->bucket_alloc);
301    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, b);
302
303    if (ap_pass_brigade(r->output_filters, bb) != APR_SUCCESS)
304        return AP_FILTER_ERROR;
305#endif
306    return OK;
307}
308
309static int file_cache_handler(request_rec *r)
310{
311    a_file *match;
312    int errstatus;
313    int rc = OK;
314
315    /* Bail out if r->handler isn't the default value, and doesn't look like a Content-Type
316     * XXX: Even though we made the user explicitly list each path to cache?
317     */ view japanese
318    if (ap_strcmp_match(r->handler, "*/*") && !AP_IS_DEFAULT_HANDLER_NAME(r->handler)) {
319        return DECLINED;
320    }
321
322    /* we don't handle anything but GET */ view japanese
323    if (r->method_number != M_GET) return DECLINED;
324
325    /* did xlat phase find the file? */ view japanese
326    match = ap_get_module_config(r->request_config, &file_cache_module);
327
328    if (match == NULL) {
329        return DECLINED;
330    }
331
332    /* note that we would handle GET on this resource */ view japanese
333    r->allowed |= (AP_METHOD_BIT << M_GET);
334
335    /* This handler has no use for a request body (yet), but we still
336     * need to read and discard it if the client sent one.
337     */ view japanese
338    if ((errstatus = ap_discard_request_body(r)) != OK)
339        return errstatus;
340
341    ap_update_mtime(r, match->finfo.mtime);
342
343    /* ap_set_last_modified() always converts the file mtime to a string
344     * which is slow.  Accelerate the common case.
345     * ap_set_last_modified(r);
346     */ view japanese
347    {
348        apr_time_t mod_time;
349        char *datestr;
350
351        mod_time = ap_rationalize_mtime(r, r->mtime);
352        if (mod_time == match->finfo.mtime)
353            datestr = match->mtimestr;
354        else {
355            datestr = apr_palloc(r->pool, APR_RFC822_DATE_LEN);
356            apr_rfc822_date(datestr, mod_time);
357        }
358        apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Last-Modified", datestr);
359    }
360
361    /* ap_set_content_length() always converts the same number and never
362     * returns an error.  Accelerate it.
363     */ view japanese
364    r->clength = match->finfo.size;
365    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Content-Length", match->sizestr);
366
367    ap_set_etag(r);
368    if ((errstatus = ap_meets_conditions(r)) != OK) {
369       return errstatus;
370    }
371
372    /* Call appropriate handler */ view japanese
373    if (!r->header_only) {
374        if (match->is_mmapped == TRUE)
375            rc = mmap_handler(r, match);
376        else
377            rc = sendfile_handler(r, match);
378    }
379
380    return rc;
381}
382
383static const command_rec file_cache_cmds[] =
384{
385AP_INIT_ITERATE("cachefile", cachefilehandle, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
386     "A space separated list of files to add to the file handle cache at config time"),
387AP_INIT_ITERATE("mmapfile", cachefilemmap, NULL, RSRC_CONF,
388     "A space separated list of files to mmap at config time"),
389    {NULL}
390};
391
392static void register_hooks(apr_pool_t *p)
393{
394    ap_hook_handler(file_cache_handler, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_LAST);
395    ap_hook_post_config(file_cache_post_config, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
396    ap_hook_translate_name(file_cache_xlat, NULL, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
397    /* This trick doesn't work apparently because the translate hooks
398       are single shot. If the core_hook returns OK, then our hook is
399       not called.
400    ap_hook_translate_name(file_cache_xlat, aszPre, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
401    */ view japanese
402
403}
404
405AP_DECLARE_MODULE(file_cache) =
406{
407    STANDARD20_MODULE_STUFF,
408    NULL,                     /* create per-directory config structure */ view japanese
409    NULL,                     /* merge per-directory config structures */ view japanese
410    create_server_config,     /* create per-server config structure */ view japanese
411    NULL,                     /* merge per-server config structures */ view japanese
412    file_cache_cmds,          /* command handlers */ view japanese
413    register_hooks            /* register hooks */ view japanese
414};

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:17-22

/*
 * 作者: mod_file_cache (Bill Stoddard <stoddard apache.org>)
 *       mod_mmap_static (Dean Gaudet <dgaudet arctic.org>) をベースに作成
 *
 * v0.01: 初回実装
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Author: mod_file_cache by Bill Stoddard <stoddard apache.org>
 *         Based on mod_mmap_static by Dean Gaudet <dgaudet arctic.org>
 *
 * v0.01: initial implementation
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:24-69

/*
    ドキュメント:

    一部のサイトでは、非常に頻繁にアクセスされる一方で、変更頻度が低い（あるいは定期的なスケジュールでしか変更されない）静的ファイル群が存在します。これらのファイルに対するオープンハンドルをキャッシュすることで、時間を節約できます。このモジュールは mod_mmap_static とは異なり、ファイルの内容ではなく、オープンファイルハンドルをキャッシュします。システムコールによるオーバーヘッドが大きく、効率的な sendfile 実装を持つシステム（Windows など）では、ファイルハンドルをキャッシュすることは、コンテンツをキャッシュするよりもいくつかの利点があります。第一に、ファイルシステムがメモリを管理できるため、アクセス頻度の低いキャッシュ済みファイルをページアウトさせることが可能です。第二に、オープンハンドルをキャッシュしてもリソースを大幅に消費しないため、サーバーの実行中に静的ファイルを動的にキャッシュへ読み込む AutoLoadCache 機能を有効にすることが可能です。ファイル変更通知機能を持つシステムでは、このモジュールを拡張して、ディスク上で変更されたキャッシュ済みファイルを自動的にガベージコレクションするようにできます。

    このモジュールは、sendfile を持つ Unix システムで動作するはずです。キャッシュするファイルを指定するには、設定ファイルに cachefile ディレクティブを記述してください。

        cachefile /path/to/file1
        cachefile /path/to/file2
        ...

    これらのファイルはサーバーの再起動時にのみキャッシュされるため、リストを変更した場合やファイル自体が変更された場合は、サーバーを再起動する必要があります。

    繰り返しになりますが、サーバーを再起動せずにファイルを「その場で（in place）」変更すると、完全に誤った内容のリクエストを返してしまう可能性があります。ファイルを更新する場合は、古いコピーを削除（unlink）してから新しいコピーを配置するようにしてください。

    これらのファイルは vhost 間などで継承されることはありません。ディレクティブはメインサーバーの設定のみに記述してください。

    既知の問題:

    これらのファイルを移動するために Alias や RewriteRule を使用しないでください。リクエストごとに余分な stat() が発生するコストを支払うことになります。これは Apache API の欠陥であり、いつか解決されることが望まれます。ファイル自体はファイルハンドルキャッシュから提供されますが、無駄な stat() が発生してしまいます。
*/

Original comment

/*
    Documentation:

    Some sites have a set of static files that are really busy, and
    change infrequently (or even on a regular schedule). Save time
    by caching open handles to these files. This module, unlike
    mod_mmap_static, caches open file handles, not file content.
    On systems (like Windows) with heavy system call overhead and
    that have an efficient sendfile implementation, caching file handles
    offers several advantages over caching content. First, the file system
    can manage the memory, allowing infrequently hit cached files to
    be paged out. Second, since caching open handles does not consume
    significant resources, it will be possible to enable an AutoLoadCache
    feature where static files are dynamically loaded in the cache
    as the server runs. On systems that have file change notification,
    this module can be enhanced to automatically garbage collect
    cached files that change on disk.

    This module should work on Unix systems that have sendfile. Place
    cachefile directives into your configuration to direct files to
    be cached.

        cachefile /path/to/file1
        cachefile /path/to/file2
        ...

    These files are only cached when the server is restarted, so if you
    change the list, or if the files are changed, then you'll need to
    restart the server.

    To reiterate that point:  if the files are modified *in place*
    without restarting the server you may end up serving requests that
    are completely bogus.  You should update files by unlinking the old
    copy and putting a new copy in place.

    There's no such thing as inheriting these files across vhosts or
    whatever... place the directives in the main server only.

    Known problems:

    Don't use Alias or RewriteRule to move these files around...  unless
    you feel like paying for an extra stat() on each request.  This is
    a deficiency in the Apache API that will hopefully be solved some day.
    The file will be served out of the file handle cache, but there will be
    an extra stat() that's a waste.
*/

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:109

/* 64ビットのファイルサイズ + null を保持するのに十分な大きさ */

Original comment

/* big enough to hold any 64-bit file size + null */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:167

/* やった、キャッシュに入れるファイルがある */

Original comment

/* WooHoo, we have a file to put in the cache */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:173-176

/* MMAPFile ディレクティブ。ファイルを MMAP します。
         * XXX: APR_HAS_LARGE_FILES の問題。サイズが MAX(apr_size_t) (おそらく 1M 以上？) を超える場合は、このリクエストを拒否する必要があります。
         */

Original comment

/* MMAPFile directive. MMAP'ing the file
         * XXX: APR_HAS_LARGE_FILES issue; need to reject this request if
         * size is greater than MAX(apr_size_t) (perhaps greater than 1M?).
         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:191

/* CacheFile ディレクティブ。ファイルハンドルをキャッシュします */

Original comment

/* CacheFile directive. Caching the file handle */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:211

/* このOSはsendfileをサポートしていません */

Original comment

/* Sendfile not supported by this OS */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:222

/* このOSはMMAPをサポートしていません */

Original comment

/* MMAP not supported by this OS */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:232

/* ふむ、ここで何かする必要があるかな？ */

Original comment

/* Hummm, anything to do here? */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:236-238

/* もしそれが自前のものであれば、余分な stat() を避けるために今 r->finfo を埋める... 
 * これは core_translate が実行された後に実行したいため、少々場当たり的な対応である。
 */

Original comment

/* If it's one of ours, fill in r->finfo now to avoid extra stat()... this is a
 * bit of a kludge, because we really want to run after core_translate runs.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:247

/* cachefile ディレクティブが少なくとも1つ使用された場合にのみ動作します */

Original comment

/* we only operate when at least one cachefile directive was used */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:257

/* キャッシュを検索 */

Original comment

/* search the cache */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:262

/* 検索結果をハンドラーに渡す */

Original comment

/* pass search results to handler */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:265

/* get_path_info() の stat() 呼び出しなどをショートサーキットする */

Original comment

/* shortcircuit the get_path_info() stat() calls and stuff */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:315-317

/* r->handler がデフォルト値ではなく、かつ Content-Type のように見えない場合は中断する
     * XXX: ユーザーがキャッシュする各パスを明示的にリストするようにしたにもかかわらずか？
     */

Original comment

/* Bail out if r->handler isn't the default value, and doesn't look like a Content-Type
     * XXX: Even though we made the user explicitly list each path to cache?
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:318-322

/*") && !AP_IS_DEFAULT_HANDLER_NAME(r->handler)) {
        return DECLINED;
    }

    /* GET 以外は処理しない */

Original comment

/*") && !AP_IS_DEFAULT_HANDLER_NAME(r->handler)) {
        return DECLINED;
    }

    /* we don't handle anything but GET */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:325

/* xlatフェーズでファイルは見つかりましたか？ */

Original comment

/* did xlat phase find the file? */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:332

/* このリソースに対するGETはここで処理することに注意 */

Original comment

/* note that we would handle GET on this resource */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:335-337

/* このハンドラーは（今のところ）リクエストボディを使用しませんが、
     * クライアントから送信された場合は読み込んで破棄する必要があります。
     */

Original comment

/* This handler has no use for a request body (yet), but we still
     * need to read and discard it if the client sent one.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:343-346

/* ap_set_last_modified() は常にファイルの mtime を文字列に変換するため、
     * 低速です。一般的なケースを高速化します。
     * ap_set_last_modified(r);
     */

Original comment

/* ap_set_last_modified() always converts the file mtime to a string
     * which is slow.  Accelerate the common case.
     * ap_set_last_modified(r);
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:361-363

/* ap_set_content_length() は常に同じ数値を変換し、エラーを返すことはない。
     * そのため高速化する。
     */

Original comment

/* ap_set_content_length() always converts the same number and never
     * returns an error.  Accelerate it.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:372

/* 適切なハンドラを呼び出す */

Original comment

/* Call appropriate handler */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/mod_file_cache.c:397-401

/* このトリックは、translateフックが一度しか実行されないため、どうやら機能しないようです。core_hookがOKを返すと、私たちのフックは呼び出されません。
    ap_hook_translate_name(file_cache_xlat, aszPre, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
    */

Original comment

/* This trick doesn't work apparently because the translate hooks
       are single shot. If the core_hook returns OK, then our hook is
       not called.
    ap_hook_translate_name(file_cache_xlat, aszPre, NULL, APR_HOOK_MIDDLE);
    */

Japanese translation
